commands.review_summary:
The PR adds fence-aware Markdown parsing to the Real behavior proof policy and regression tests for fenced proof headings and missing-proof phrases.

PR surface: Tests +59, Other +58. Total +117 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the original current-main truncation path and the current PR false-pass path: fenced `What was not tested` content like `- not tested` is stripped to a bulleted string that misses `missingValueRegex`, while the whole-section scan skips fenced lines.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer handling because the code blocker is concrete, but the required external PR proof is insufficient and must be refreshed on the current head.

target.title: fix(ci): ignore fenced headings in proof parser
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens the Real behavior proof parser so Markdown-looking headings and proof-like text copied inside fenced terminal output do not end, replace, or invalidate the proof section early.

This keeps the existing proof field requirements, mock-only checks, and current-main bounded GitHub API response handling intact. The parser now uses fence tracking for proof-section discovery, proof-section slicing, per-field value extraction, label stripping for whole-section scans, and the standalone missing-proof scan. Headings and copied phrases inside backtick or tilde fences stay transcript content; real PR-body headings and proof-field labels still delimit sections and fields outside fences. Field-level missing-value checks ignore Markdown fence wrapper lines so actual fenced `What was not tested` values containing missing-proof phrases are still rejected.

## Context

PR #87341 exposed the original bug: its real behavior proof copied a generated prompt that contained `## TOOLS.md` inside a fenced output block. The parser treated that line as the next PR-body heading and could miss later proof fields.

Follow-up review found related edges in the same parser path: same-length info-string fences like ```` ```js ```` are content inside an outer transcript, copied headings such as `## Real behavior proof` must not become new proof sections, copied field-template lines such as `Observed result: not tested` must not become field starts, copied standalone phrases such as `not tested` inside fenced output must not trigger the missing-proof rejection, copied field-template lines whose value is a bare fence such as ```` Observed result: ``` ```` must not mutate fence state, actual fenced missing-proof field values must still be rejected, and current-main `readBoundedGitHubApiJson` behavior must be preserved.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Real behavior proof sections and fields keep parseable content after copied terminal output that contains Markdown headings, proof-field-looking lines, bare-fence field-template lines, and standalone missing-proof phrases such as `not tested`; actual proof fields whose fenced value contains missing-proof text are rejected; the current-main bounded GitHub API response helper remains present after the rebase.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout worktree `.worktrees/87390-proof-parser-fence-aware-section` on macOS, branch `codex/proof-parser-fence-aware-section`, commit `ba0132c5c6324a7d69d1aff56e329fd401d97992`, Node `v26.0.0`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebased the single PR commit onto current `origin/main`, verified `readBoundedGitHubApiJson` and oversized-response tests are still present, ran direct local node smokes against `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs`, then ran the focused policy test file with the repo Vitest wrapper.

Evidence after fix: `rg -n "readBoundedGitHubApiJson|rejects oversized" scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts` shows the bounded helper, membership call, and oversized-response tests still present. The direct parser smoke for copied fenced headings returned `status: passed` with `evidenceHasLiveCommand: true`. The direct smoke for fenced missing-proof field values returned `missing notTested` for `not tested`, `did not test`, and `could not test`. Focused regression tests passed 1 file / 33 tests.

Observed result after fix: The PR no longer drops current-main bounded GitHub API response handling, `evaluateRealBehaviorProof` still ignores copied proof-template lines inside fenced output, and fenced missing-proof field values are rejected instead of labeling that proof sufficient.

What was not tested: Live GitHub Actions proof-gate rerun on commit `ba0132c5c6324a7d69d1aff56e329fd401d97992`. External autoreview was not rerun because the escalation was rejected by policy for sending workspace diff data to an external review engine; this closeout used local source inspection plus focused local parser proof instead.

Direct parser smoke output:

```text
{
  "status": "passed",
  "observedResult": "Parser kept the real field below the transcript.",
  "evidenceHasLiveCommand": true
}
not tested missing notTested
did not test missing notTested
could not test missing notTested
```

## Verification

- `node --input-type=module` direct parser smoke against `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts` (1 file / 33 tests passed)
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Refs #87341
target.author: monkeyleet
